question: What are the two types of object metadata in Amazon S3?
supporting_sentences: There are two kinds of object metadata in Amazon S3: system-defined metadata and user-defined metadata.

question: Can user-defined metadata be modified after an object is uploaded to Amazon S3?
answer: No, the only way to modify user-defined metadata is to make a copy of the object and set the metadata.
supporting_sentences: After you upload the object, you can't modify this user-defined metadata. The only way to modify this metadata is to make a copy of the object and set the metadata.

question: What is the purpose of S3 Metadata in Amazon S3?
answer: S3 Metadata accelerates data discovery by automatically capturing metadata for objects in general purpose buckets and storing it in read-only, fully managed Apache Iceberg tables that you can query.
supporting_sentences: S3 Metadata accelerates data discovery by automatically capturing metadata for the objects in your general purpose buckets and storing it in read-only, fully managed Apache Iceberg tables that you can query.

question: Which system-defined metadata in Amazon S3 can users modify?
answer: Users can modify system metadata such as Cache-Control, Content-Disposition, Content-Encoding, Content-Type, x-amz-server-side-encryption, x-amz-checksum-type, x-amz-storage-class, x-amz-website-redirect-location, x-amz-server-side-encryption-aws-kms-key-id, and x-amz-server-side-encryption-customer-algorithm.
supporting_sentences: User controlled – Other system metadata, such as the storage class configured for the object and whether the object has server-side encryption enabled, are examples of system metadata whose values you control.
The following table provides a list of system-defined metadata and whether you can update it.

question: What prefix must user-defined metadata names begin with when uploading objects using the REST API in Amazon S3?
answer: User-defined metadata names must begin with 'x-amz-meta-'.
supporting_sentences: When you upload objects using the REST API, the optional user-defined metadata names must begin with x-amz-meta- to distinguish them from other HTTP headers.
